market_session: US regular session close; AP week-to-date basis through June 18

calculation.method: reported week-to-date point and percent changes from the AP markets wire

text: For the week through the June 18 close, the AP reported the S&P 500 up 69.12 points, or 0.9%; the Dow up 362.44 points, or 0.7%; the Nasdaq up 629.09 points, or 2.4%; and the Russell 2000 up 35.77 points, or 1.2%.

market_session: US regular session close; AP year-to-date basis

calculation.method: reported year-to-date point and percent changes from the AP markets wire

text: For the year through the June 18 close, the AP reported the S&P 500 up 655.08 points, or 9.6%; the Dow up 3,501.41 points, or 7.3%; the Nasdaq up 3,275.94 points, or 14.1%; and the Russell 2000 up 497.86 points, or 20.1%.

The United States and Iran signed an initial 14-point memorandum of understanding to end the war, reopen the Strait of Hormuz to oil tanker traffic, and extend the current ceasefire for 60 days, with a formal signing ceremony originally planned for Friday in Switzerland; the deal drove a broad risk-on rally in US equities.

The yield on the 10-year US Treasury note fell to 4.44% on Thursday, partially reversing the prior session's increase, while the 2-year yield edged up to 4.20%; the AP reported Treasury yields eased as investors digested the Fed's hawkish projections alongside the US-Iran deal.

Crude oil prices wavered after the United States and Iran signed an agreement to end their war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz to oil tanker traffic, with the AP reporting CL=F down 0.67% on the session.
US markets were scheduled to be closed on Friday June 19, 2026 for the Juneteenth federal holiday, making June 18 the final trading day of the week.
